nodes.body:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Redact local checkout paths from proof artifacts**

In this public SDK repo, the Definition of Done requires no hardcoded absolute paths, but this committed proof snapshot includes a local Windows home/worktree path; the same pattern also appears in the install/import proof logs. This leaks a contributor-specific path and makes the proof artifact non-portable, so replace these with repo-relative paths or a redacted placeholder before committing the run artifacts.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

The regression assertion `budget_events[0]["data"]["total_cost_usd"] == 0.051` is brittle because this example’s costs come from `estimate_cost()` (model pricing tables can change, and float rendering/rounding can differ slightly). Consider asserting with a tolerance (e.g., approx) and/or deriving the expected total from the trace’s per-turn `cost_usd` values instead of hard-coding 0.051.

This test checks that `data.cost_usd` is absent, but `_extract_cost()` also prefers a top-level `cost_usd`. To fully guard against reintroducing double-counting, also assert that the `guard.budget_exceeded` event itself does not include a top-level `cost_usd` field.

This proof summary hard-codes an absolute local checkout path including a Windows username. For public/portable proof artifacts, consider redacting user-specific paths (e.g., use a relative path from repo root or a placeholder) to avoid leaking personal info and to keep the proof reproducible across environments.
